Provides a backlink from the plan issue to the workflow that generated it. docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:61:- Quick navigation from plan issue to the workflow that created it docs/learned/planning/plan-backend-migration.md:18:When exec scripts need to read or update plan issues, use `PlanBackend` instead of direct `GitHubIssues` calls. PlanBackend provides typed abstractions with discriminated union error handling. docs/learned/planning/planned-pr-context-local-preprocessing.md:53:**Graceful degradation**: If no PR exists for the plan issue, comment fetching is skipped entirely. Missing PR comments don't block the learn workflow — sessions alone contain sufficient material for insight extraction. docs/learned/planning/consolidation-labels.md:34:The label application happens in Step 7 of `/erk:replan`, after the new plan issue is created via `/erk:plan-save`. docs/learned/planning/learn-pipeline-workflow.md:27:The learn pipeline has 7 stages that transform raw session JSONL into a documentation plan issue. The key architectural decision is **where processing happens**: stages 1-5 run locally (fast, free), while stages 6-7 run in GitHub Actions (background, billed). docs/learned/planning/branch-name-inference.md:32:When code needs to look up a PR from a plan issue, `branch_name` may be missing from metadata. The system uses two layers: docs/learned/planning/token-optimization-patterns.md:40:The `/erk:replan` command demonstrates this pattern. Step 3 explicitly skips content fetching ("Plan content is fetched by each Explore agent in Step 4, not in the main context"), and Step 4 launches parallel Explore agents that each fetch their own plan issue as their first action. docs/learned/planning/complete-inventory-protocol.md:10: - action: "closing a plan issue without verifying all items were addressed" docs/learned/planning/complete-inventory-protocol.md:35:| Before closing a plan issue as "complete" | The only reliable completion check is comparing actual files against planned items |

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: When exec scripts need to read or update plan issues, use `PlanBackend` instead of direct `GitHubIssues` calls. PlanBackend provides typed abstractions with discriminated union error handling.

session.tool_result: docs/learned/planning/lifecycle.md:636: - `objective-context` marker: stores objective issue number (read by plan-save to link the plan to its parent objective) docs/learned/planning/objective-update-after-land.md:20:After landing a PR, the associated objective issue needs updating. This is handled by the `objective-update-after-land` exec script. docs/learned/planning/one-shot-workflow.md:83: - `OBJECTIVE_ISSUE` -- objective issue number (if from roadmap)

docs/learned/planning/learn-pipeline-workflow.md:60:Finds all session logs related to a plan by querying GitHub issue metadata for tracked session IDs, then resolving those IDs to local file paths. Three session source types exist: docs/learned/planning/branch-name-inference.md:24:1. **Plan saved** → GitHub issue created (no branch exists yet) docs/learned/planning/lifecycle.md:61: GitHub Issue git branch GitHub Actions Code Changes Issue Closed docs/learned/planning/lifecycle.md:197:4. `/erk:plan-save` creates GitHub Issue with `erk-plan` label docs/learned/planning/lifecycle.md:207:This creates a GitHub Issue directly from the plan file. docs/learned/planning/lifecycle.md:211:Plans are stored in GitHub Issues: docs/learned/planning/lifecycle.md:405:| `issue_number` | GitHub issue number to implement | docs/learned/planning/lifecycle.md:483:3. Posts start comment to GitHub issue (if linked) docs/learned/planning/lifecycle.md:715: description: "GitHub issue number to implement" docs/learned/planning/lifecycle.md:926:- Plan exists only as a GitHub issue with `erk-plan` label docs/learned/planning/session-deduplication.md:22:When Claude retries a blocked ExitPlanMode call, the same plan could be saved multiple times, creating duplicate GitHub issues. This can happen when: docs/learned/planning/workflow.md:133:- Links to the GitHub issue for progress tracking docs/learned/planning/workflow.md:341:- `erk implement <issue>` - Implement plan from existing GitHub issue in current directory docs/learned/planning/pr-submission-patterns.md:33:**Session-level:** Plan-save operations use scratch-directory marker files keyed by session ID. Before creating a GitHub issue, the command checks if this session already created one. This prevents the specific failure mode where exit-plan-mode hook retries cause duplicate plans. docs/learned/planning/pr-discovery.md:42:When `branch_name` is unavailable, the GitHub issues timeline API finds PRs that cross-reference the issue. This uses the `get_prs_referencing_issue()` gateway method, which filters timeline events for `cross-referenced` entries. docs/learned/planning/index.md:35:- **[metadata-block-fallback.md](metadata-block-fallback.md)** — extracting plan content from GitHub issue comments, debugging 'no plan content found' errors in replan or plan-implement, working with older erk-plans that lack metadata blocks docs/learned/planning/metadata-block-fallback.md:6: - "extracting plan content from GitHub issue comments" docs/learned/planning/tripwire-promotion-workflow.md:46:Posts candidates to GitHub issue as a metadata comment with key `tripwire-candidates`. docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:72:`learned_from_issue` is the **storage-layer** field name (in the plan-header metadata block written to GitHub issue/PR bodies). `learn_plan_issue` is the **application-layer** field name (on the `Plan` dataclass used in pipelines). docs/learned/planning/session-preprocessing.md:71:The learn workflow needs to know which sessions belong to which plan. This linkage is stored in GitHub issue metadata, not in the session files themselves: docs/learned/planning/one-shot-workflow.md:138:5. Saves plan to GitHub issue: docs/learned/planning/plan-execution-patterns.md:46:3. If the plan has a GitHub issue, the PR links to it

session.assistant: Good! The changes in the planning directory look correct. Let me now see a sample of the actual changes to verify they're what we intended:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Show actual changes in one file

session.tool_result:
diff --git a/docs/learned/planning/no-changes-handling.md b/docs/learned/planning/no-changes-handling.md index 0f412541f..4093218dd 100644 --- a/docs/learned/planning/no-changes-handling.md +++ b/docs/learned/planning/no-changes-handling.md @@ -27,7 +27,7 @@ Instead of failing, the workflow: 1. **Detects no changes**: Checks if the branch has any commits beyond the base 2. **Creates diagnostic PR**: Updates the PR body with diagnostic information 3. **Applies label**: Adds `no-changes` label (orange, `#FFA500`) -4. **Posts issue comment**: Notifies the plan issue with a link to the PR +4. **Posts issue comment**: Notifies the plan with a link to the PR 5. **Exits gracefully**: Returns exit code 0 (success)
## Label Definition @@ -52,7 +52,7 @@ The diagnostic PR body includes: When encountering a no-changes PR:
1. **Review recent commits**: Check if the work appears in trunk -2. **If already done**: Close both the PR and the linked plan issue +2. **If already done**: Close both the PR and the linked plan 3. **If not done**: Investigate why no changes were produced (plan may need revision)
## Exit Code Semantics
session.assistant: Perfect! Let me verify one more file to ensure the changes are correct:
session.tool_result:
diff --git a/docs/learned/planning/cross-artifact-analysis.md b/docs/learned/planning/cross-artifact-analysis.md index ba3219c09..5ce3ae1e0 100644 --- a/docs/learned/planning/cross-artifact-analysis.md +++ b/docs/learned/planning/cross-artifact-analysis.md @@ -18,7 +18,7 @@ Plans and PRs can have complex relationships:
| Relationship | Description | Detection Method | | ------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | -| Linked | PR explicitly references plan issue | PR body contains `Plan: #<issue>` reference | +| Linked | PR explicitly references plan | PR body contains `Plan: #<issue>` reference | | Superseding | PR implements work that makes plan obsolete | Compare PR changes with plan intent | | Partial | PR implements subset of plan scope | Evidence-based analysis of overlap | | Independent | PR and plan address unrelated concerns | No meaningful overlap in changes |

session.tool_result: docs/learned/planning/branch-name-inference.md:When code needs to look up a PR from a plan issue, `branch_name` may be missing from metadata. The system uses two layers: docs/learned/planning/learn-pipeline-workflow.md:The learn pipeline has 7 stages that transform raw session JSONL into a documentation plan issue. The key architectural decision is **where processing happens**: stages 1-5 run locally (fast, free), while stages 6-7 run in GitHub Actions (background, billed). docs/learned/planning/learn-pipeline-workflow.md:Finds all session logs related to a plan issue by querying GitHub issue metadata for tracked session IDs, then resolving those IDs to local file paths. Three session source types exist: docs/learned/planning/learn-pipeline-workflow.md:Human reviews the learn plan issue, optionally edits it, and submits for implementation through the standard `plan-implement` workflow. docs/learned/planning/plan-backend-migration.md:When exec scripts need to read or update plan issues, use `PlanBackend` instead of direct `GitHubIssues` calls. PlanBackend provides typed abstractions with discriminated union error handling. docs/learned/planning/plan-backend-migration.md:This pattern ensures the gist URL is still reported even if the plan issue update fails. docs/learned/planning/plan-backend-migration.md:`get_metadata_field()` returns `PlanNotFound` when the plan issue doesn't exist. `update_metadata()` also returns `PlanNotFound`. However, the semantics differ: docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:GitHub Actions workflow run URL that created this plan. Provides a backlink from the plan issue to the workflow that generated it. docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:- Quick navigation from plan issue to the workflow that created it docs/learned/planning/complete-inventory-protocol.md: - action: "closing a plan issue without verifying all items were addressed" docs/learned/planning/complete-inventory-protocol.md:| Before closing a plan issue as "complete" | The only reliable completion check is comparing actual files against planned items | docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-validation.md:Learn plans exist to extract insights from **implementation work** (erk-plan issues). A learn plan must never target another learn plan — this would create documentation cycles where plans endlessly analyze each other instead of analyzing real code changes. docs/learned/planning/consolidation-labels.md:The label application happens in Step 7 of `/erk:replan`, after the new plan issue is created via `/erk:plan-save`. docs/learned/planning/plan-header-privatization.md:The `plan_header.py` module contains low-level functions for reading/writing plan issue metadata blocks. These are being gradually privatized behind the `PlanBackend` abstraction. docs/learned/planning/cross-artifact-analysis.md:| Linked | PR explicitly references plan issue | PR body contains `Plan: #<issue>` reference | docs/learned/planning/no-changes-handling.md:4. **Posts issue comment**: Notifies the plan issue with a link to the PR docs/learned/planning/no-changes-handling.md:2. **If already done**: Close both the PR and the linked plan issue
session.assistant: I need to make these remaining edits. Let me continue:

tool_use.param: When code needs to look up a PR from a plan issue, `branch_name` may be missing from metadata. The system uses two layers:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: When exec scripts need to read or update plan issues, use `PlanBackend` instead of direct `GitHubIssues` calls. PlanBackend provides typed abstractions with discriminated union error handling.
When exec scripts need to read or update plans, use `PlanBackend` instead of direct `GitHubIssues` calls. PlanBackend provides typed abstractions with discriminated union error handling.

tool_use.param: This pattern ensures the gist URL is still reported even if the plan issue update fails.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
This field enables:
- Debugging failed plan creations by linking to the workflow run - Tr...[truncated 6 chars]...tically generated plans - Quick navigation from plan issue to the workflow that created it

This field enables:
- Debugging failed plan creations by linking to the workflow run - Tracing provenance of automatically generated plans - Quick navigation from plan to the workflow that created it

tool_use.param: tripwires: - action: "estimating effort for a plan without checking actual files changed...[truncated 246 chars]...ainst the plan's items before closing. Silent omissions are the most common failure mode."
